ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "is_2fa_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if two-factor authentication is enabled */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activations" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activation request */,
  "activated" INTEGER /* Status indicating if the account is activated */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the activation request */,
  "updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting activation */,
  "activation_key" TEXT /* Unique activation key for the account */,
  "activated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who activated the account */,
  "activation_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the activation request was made */,
  "activation_device" TEXT /* Device used for activation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activations_activated_by FOREIGN KEY ("activated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activation_keys" (
  "key_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activation key */,
  "activation_key" TEXT /* The activation key itself */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the key belongs */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the activation key is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the key was created */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Expiration timestamp for the activation key */,
  "used_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the key was last used */,
  "used_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the key was used */,
  "used_device" TEXT /* Device used to activate the key */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("key_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activation_keys_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON column to store a list of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activation_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activation_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, failed) */,
  "retry_count" INTEGER /* Number of retries attempted if the notification failed */,
  "last_retry" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last retry attempt */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reset_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting the reset */,
  "request_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the reset was requested */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reset request (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "request_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the reset request was made */,
  "request_device" TEXT /* Device used for the reset request */,
  "reset_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who completed the reset (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reset_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reset_requests_reset_by FOREIGN KEY ("reset_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user associated with the session */,
  "login_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user logged in */,
  "logout_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user logged out */,
  "session_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the session was started */,
  "session_device" TEXT /* Device used for the session */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "email_verifications" (
  "verification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each verification record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being verified */,
  "is_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the email is verified */,
  "verification_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when verification occurred */,
  "verification_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the verification was completed */,
  "verification_device" TEXT /* Device used for verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("verification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_email_verifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting the reset */,
  "reset_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the reset was requested */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reset request (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "reset_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the password reset was completed */,
  "reset_device" TEXT /* Device used for the password reset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role associated with the permission */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Description of the permission granted to the role */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the permission is a default permission for the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_trail" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user whose account was changed */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., activation, update) */,
  "change_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the change occurred */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to make the change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trail_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_trail_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the setting is active */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
